author: James Carter
title: High Protein Cottage Cheese Cinnamon Roll Bread
ingredients: 2 cups oat flour (or almond flour for gluten-free)
⅓ cup coconut sugar or brown sugar
2 tbsp melted butter or coconut oil
Optional Glaze: ½ cup powdered sugar
Optional Glaze: 1–2 tbsp blended cottage cheese or Greek yogurt
Optional Glaze: Splash of vanilla or milk
2 cups oat flour (or almond flour for gluten-free)
⅓ cup coconut sugar or brown sugar
2 tbsp melted butter or coconut oil
Optional Glaze: ½ cup powdered sugar
Optional Glaze: 1–2 tbsp blended cottage cheese or Greek yogurt
Optional Glaze: Splash of vanilla or milk

instructions_list: Preheat your oven to 350°F. Grease a loaf pan and line it with parchment paper for easy removal.
Place the cottage cheese into a blender or food processor and blend until completely smooth to ensure a soft texture.
In a large bowl, whisk together the smooth cottage cheese, egg, maple syrup (or honey), and vanilla extract until well combined.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the oat flour, baking powder, baking soda, and a pinch of salt.
Stir the dry ingredients into the wet mixture until a thick batter forms.
In a small bowl, mix the cinnamon swirl ingredients: coconut sugar (or brown sugar), melted butter, and cinnamon until it forms a paste.
Pour half of the batter into the loaf pan. Spoon half of the cinnamon mixture over it and swirl gently with a knife.
Add the remaining batter and the rest of the cinnamon mixture. Swirl again to create a marble pattern.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let cool in the pan.
Optional: Mix powdered sugar, cottage cheese/yogurt, and milk to make a glaze and drizzle over the cooled bread.
description: A soft, moist high-protein bread that tastes just like a cinnamon roll. This easy blender recipe packs over 20g of protein per slice for a perfect quick breakfast.
